violations.text: Many flies observed in the produce room near the produce compactor. No food products observed to be affected. Pest control is to be called to assess and treat this problem. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

The door to the outside, located in the produce walk-in cooler, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the deli 3-door freezer in the prep area. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The coving in the deli area by the walk-in cooler and the meat room area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2025

The door frame in the meat walk-in cooler were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Door frame is to be replaced or resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2025

Protective shielding was observed to be cracked or missing for the lights in the produce walk-in cooler. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Cut watermelon wedges was observed held at temperatures ranging from 54-57 degrees Fahrenheit in the produce open air case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cut melons were evaluated and were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Establishment shall not use the produce open air case to store TCS food items until an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less is maintained. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form (Shellstock). Consumer advisory is required. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

Garlic parmesan sauce was observed thawing in the deli/prepared foods area prep sink without running water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food items were observed at 60 degrees Fahrenheit and were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Swordfish steaks were observed thawing in the seafood walk-in cooler with intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood items packaged in ROP shall be removed from their original packaging prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen into the food item. Food items were observed still partially frozen and were removed from their packaging during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the pizza display case refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

Fly tape observed in the produce room. Tape observed to have many flies and was near food products. Fly tape was removed and is not to be used to control fly issue. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Many flies observed in the produce room near the produce compactor. No food products observed to be affected. Pest control is to be called to assess and treat this problem. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The door to the outside, located in the produce walk-in cooler, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Condensation was observed dripping from the evaporator fans onto food items in meat walk-in cooler. Food items were evaluated and moved to different storage location. Establishment is to refrain from storing food items near potential source of contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the seafood area. Single service items were placed into protective packaging during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025
Several fans of walk-in cooler evaporator units were observed to be missing fan blades or are no longer operational and were not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The produce open air case located in the produce area was observed to not hold an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less and is not in good repair. Establishment is not to store TCS food items in unit until an ambient of 41 degrees or less is maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the deli walk-in cooler, 2-door deli reach-in freezer, seafood walk-in cooler, and the produce walk-in cooler. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The stand mixer located in the bakery area was observed to be leaking from the internal gear case and is not in good repair. Unit is to be taken out of service unit. Establishment is to repair or replace unit. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

The 2-door reach-in located in the deli area was not in good repair. The lower shelf was rusted and no longer cleanable. Lower shelf is to be covered with material that is smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent, or refrigerator is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following areas have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:- Ceiling in the deli walk-in cooler- The floor and display case door tracks in the seafood area- The probe wire coming from ceiling in the meat walk-in cooler- The ceiling and walls in the dairy walk-in cooler-The bakery walk-in cooler fan guardsNon-food contact surfaces of equipment and facilities shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

A hose is connected to a faucet under the 3-bay sink in the deli preparation area. This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The grout was observed to be low and in the deli preparation area and several floor tiles were observed to be cracked or missing in the cheese area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026

The wall by the freezer in the bakery area and the door frame in the meat walk-in cooler were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The dry storage in the back mezzanine area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025
The coving in the deli area by the walk-in cooler and the meat room area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2025

Protective shielding was observed to be cracked or missing for the lights in the produce walk-in cooler. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Fly tape observed in the produce room. Tape observed to have many flies and was near food products. Fly tape was removed and is not to be used to control fly issue. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Many flies observed in the produce room near the produce compactor. No food products observed to be affected. Pest control is to be called to assess and treat this problem. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The 2-door reach-in located in the deli area was not in good repair. The lower shelf was rusted and no longer cleanable. Lower shelf is to be covered with material that is smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent, or refrigerator is to be replaced. In addition, one of the condenser units in a walk-in cooler is not in good repair. Two of the fans are missing and the remaining two are not covered/shielded. Condenser unit is to be properly repaired. The gaskets of some of the refrigerated units are in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine, the lower shelves throughout the store, the drains throughout the store (resulting in stagnant water), and the vents of the open refrigerated units throughout the store, have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
